title: Test Harness For Hosted Runtime
use_for: testing domain logic, modules, and pipelines without a full host runtime
designing fake adapters, clocks, dispatchers, configuration, and diagnostics for tests
separating deterministic tests from live-host smoke checks
supporting greenfield rewrites with fast validation loops
do_not_use_for: testing exact host rendering or input behavior
rules: test domain mechanics and pipeline behavior through stable contracts
fake host adapters at boundaries rather than mocking internals
provide deterministic time, configuration, dispatch, and diagnostics where needed
keep live-host smoke tests small and separate from offline tests
test degraded and failure states explicitly
procedure: Identify which runtime surfaces can be tested offline and which require live host smoke checks.
Define fake or stub implementations for host APIs, clocks, dispatchers, configuration, and diagnostics.
Expose module and pipeline entry points through contracts suitable for test harness use.
Build scenarios around inputs, lifecycle events, commands, and published outputs.
Add degraded integration and failure cases.
Keep harness utilities small enough that they do not become a second runtime.

primary_when: the task is primarily about testing a hosted runtime without the full host environment
must_not_own_when: the task is primarily about live host hook discovery or UI layout

solves: how to validate a hosted runtime in fast offline loops
how to avoid requiring a game or host process for every domain test
how to test degraded and failure behavior at boundaries

core_principles: Offline tests should exercise contracts, not private implementation layout.
Host fakes belong at adapters and boundary contracts.
Live smoke tests prove integration; offline harnesses prove design behavior.
A harness should reduce runtime assumptions, not duplicate the whole runtime.
constraints: Do not fake internals when a boundary fake is possible.
Do not make tests rely on real time, random ordering, or live host objects unless intentionally testing them.
Do not let harness-only APIs leak into production design.
Do not bind reusable test guidance to a named project or path.
decision_rules: If code can run from snapshots and commands, test it offline.
If code requires host object identity or rendering, cover it with smoke tests.
If a module depends on lifecycle, provide test lifecycle events.
If a feature degrades when integration is missing, add a missing-integration test.
If the harness becomes complex, simplify production boundaries first.

avoid_patterns: flaky tests based on real timing

recipes.name: Build an offline runtime harness
recipes.when: Use when a hosted runtime needs fast tests without launching the host.
steps: Identify host boundaries and replace them with fakes.
Provide deterministic clock, dispatch, config, and diagnostics.
Drive modules with explicit lifecycle events.
Keep a separate smoke path for live host integration.

pitfalls: Testing private implementation instead of contracts.
Letting the harness hide bad production boundaries.
Skipping live smoke tests for fragile integrations.
Allowing real-time behavior to make tests flaky.
review_checklist: Are host dependencies faked at boundaries?
Do tests assert contracts and published outputs?
Is live smoke coverage separated from offline tests?

references.title: Contracts
references.interpretation: Use for testable boundaries and assertions.
references.why: Hosted runtime tests should target contracts.

references.title: Runtime Lifecycle
references.interpretation: Use to drive lifecycle phases in test harnesses.
references.why: Modules and runtime owners need deterministic lifecycle tests.
